title: What am I assigned to that isn't already done (Personal to-do list/My Week)
excerpt:
An obvious feature that is still missing in Monday is the personal to-do list. Across all boards, what are the pulses that I’m assigned to that are not done yet? 
There is the “assigned to me” saved search in Search Ever…

posts.author: Foobart (Bart Breeschoten)
posts.cookedHtml:
An obvious feature that is still missing in Monday is the personal to-do list. Across all boards, what are the pulses that I’m assigned to that are not done yet?

There is the “assigned to me” saved search in Search Everything, but that doesn’t let you filter out all the ‘done’ pulses and is therefore not usable as a to-do list.

There’s also the ‘show assignments without a date’ in the My Week settings, but that also includes the pulses with a ‘done’ status.

The feature request https://community.monday.com/t/unassign-when-issue-is-set-to-done/527 seeks a solution for the same problem.

My approach would be to make Search Everything more powerful. This could be achieved by implementing a UI filter option that lets you include or exclude pulse states.
Another way is to allow for Search Everything search strings with parameters, for instance:
`

“John Doe -status:done”.

`
Stored as a saved search, this would always show you your personal to-do list.

posts.author: z-hurst (Zach)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve been looking something similar to this too. Currently I’d like a way to do a search on a specific tag and exclude ‘done’ items.
Ability to exclude statuses or other data from search results would be fantastic for so many uses.

posts.author: rick (Rick Castorani)
posts.cookedHtml: Count me as one of the people that tried using the “-” modifier in hopes that it would act like Google and remove the item from the search query. Would LOVE to see this.

posts.author: alejencia (Alex Becerra)
posts.cookedHtml:
Kind of and sort of like this …

I was thinking more of personal dashboard - user activity

Dashboards - User Activity Feature requests
Hi! as an Operations Manager I’ve been looking for ways to quantify and review my teams activity and have been relying on the Search Everything to see what each individual team member has on their plate. I was hoping to have a better way via the dashboards to have an overview of an user. The below is a quick visual of what I mean. Ideally I’d select an user and after I select the columns to count the dashboard would provide me a summary of ALL boards for that person to give me a very HIGH leve…

posts.author: Eva_McCann (Eva McCann)
posts.cookedHtml:
What am I assigned to that isn’t already done: my personal to-do list
That’s exactly what we have been trying to create… filters for task owners only work within boards. if they could work across boards that would be perfect.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml: Do you not find My Week helpful here?

posts.author: Eva_McCann (Eva McCann)
posts.cookedHtml: Unfortunately My Week shows everybody’s tasks due…

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can change My Week to see “Just Me” using the person filter at the top:

Screen Shot 2019-08-20 at 18.16.02.png1436×859 105 KB

Might that work for you?

posts.author: Eva_McCann (Eva McCann)
posts.cookedHtml: Unfortunately that still shows pulses that I’m part of but don’t own. There doesn’t seem a way to see just the pulses I am the Owner of…

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml: When you say “own” as opposed to “part of” what are you referring to? Do you have more than one people column?

posts.author: Eva_McCann (Eva McCann)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, we use Owner for the person who’s accountable to get it done and People for the team that may be involved or need to be kept informed.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml: Can you send us a screenshot of your board and also My Week with your settings open so I can see better and try and see if I can find a solution?

posts.author: alejencia (Alex Becerra)
posts.cookedHtml:
Julia,

I might have jumped in a bit late on this one … but want to give my two cents on the topic.

We have an onboarding board that is comprised of several PEOPLE columns all in ONE board. For example, the first people column is the “HIRING MANAGER” the next one is the “BUDGET ANALYST” the third is the “SYSTEMS ADMINISTRATOR” the fourth “THE TRAINER” … The hiring Manager people column in our “workflow” is really the only person that should OWN the PULSE, all the others are there because automations will ASSIGN them after other events (Statuses) have occurred, and YET when we use the MY WEEK, other PEOPLE Columns staff get the pulse in their view when in reality it should only be the “HIRING MANAGER” Person that we’d like to own that pulse. I almost envision at the BOARD LEVEL a switch / Setting that asks (WHICH people column OWNS the pulse?) so that when using MY WEEK, it ONLY Picks up that column only and ignores the rest.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for adding your experiences. We’ll send this as feedback for consideration

posts.cookedHtml:

posts.author: jaguilar (Josh Aguilar)

Any progress on this feature request? I too would love to see a personal to-do list that aggregates all items that I am “assigned to” which are NOT marked as complete!

posts.author: nwilczek (Niles)
posts.cookedHtml:
Can someone from Monday.com explain to me how they created a task tracking tool that is lacking the most fundamental feature, a list of my “to-do’s?” It absolutely blows my mind.

Someone at my company decided to switch to Monday.com, but now every single team member is asking “How to do I see my tasks?” Seems pretty basic!!!

posts.author: JohnMonte7 (John Monte)
posts.cookedHtml: I couldn’t agree with you more. I’m hoping this happens ASAP. Personal tasks + reoccurring tasks are a vital part of my own workflow.

posts.author: pkortman (Paul Kortman)
posts.cookedHtml:
I came here looking for an answer to this and found that the search everything function has teammates images (including mine) and I can just click on their image (or mine) to see everything assigned to them or to me. This works perfectly for me to be able to see everything that is assigned to me. (or to my teammates) See the red arrow below for where I see the simple/easy search which I can then save and have as a bookmark.

Screenshot 2020-02-18 09.39.431382×1950 267 KB

posts.author: Foobart (Bart Breeschoten)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi pkortman, I know about this option but the problem is that it shows all items that I’m assigned to, including the ones that were completed (status Done) months ago. It is of no use if you want a clean list of items that are assigned to you and still need some work, i.e.: your personal across-all-boards to-do list.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Foobart:

the problem is that it shows all items that I’m assigned to, including the ones that were completed (status Done) months ago.

That’s the issue. In this discussion we want to see: “What am I assigned to that isn’t already done”

Search Everything merely shows everything with our name on it. It does not serve as a clean personal to-do list.

posts.author: Burt2Burt (Bart Brinkman)
posts.cookedHtml: Exactly the same problem here, we are fully operational on this platform, but this could be a serious dealbreaker. Please add this feature.

posts.author: StephanieSJohnson (Stephanie Still Johnson)
posts.cookedHtml: I just upgraded this account and am in shock that this basic feature is not available. There is absolutely no way I can onboard my entire team if they cannot see their to-do listing (minus anything already completed) in order by due date for all boards in one place.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Burt2Burt @StephanieSJohnson Don’t forget to add your vote using the Vote button at the top of the page.

It’s one of the metrics used by the Monday.com team to assess the popularity of individual feature requests. Helps them prioritize.

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
Definitely voted for this one!

If there was an automation to unassign when status is set to done i would be kinda happy with the overview ‘everything assigned to me’ in the search all section.

But to be honest i think Monday should offer a better overview of this in the My week section or so

posts.author: egilchrist (Evan Gilchrist)
posts.cookedHtml: I created a community account just to vote for this- this is a huge issue for adopting this platform. The whole idea is to simplify the tasks assigned to an individual- the fact that you have to toggle between boards to get a clean picture of what needs to get done defeats the point of that. It seems there are many resources being invested in integrations right now, but I would say this just table stakes for a project management platform. Before you start building out these specific use case capabilities, this is a fundamental need!

posts.author: luke.kean (luke.kean@medcast.com.au)
posts.cookedHtml: Bizarre that a task tracking tool lacks the most fundamental feature, a list of my “to-do’s”. It absolutely blows my mind. I’m creating separate JIRA tasks to keep track and prioritize my Monday tasks.

posts.author: bookit (Amy Harris)
posts.cookedHtml: So can anyone at Monday reply and give us a timeline for this? It is crucial.

posts.author: kclutton (Kate)
posts.cookedHtml: I also created a community account just to vote for this. It is strange that a project management app dosen’t have this feature. I work on just about every project across my organisation and it is a nightmare to keep track of my tasks.

This is critical. We’re evaluating now, and are in the chaos of another system that has the same problem (among a massive list of others). Working cross-project and cross-client, we need to have a view that our employees can view across all work with the ability to sort/filter.

Basically, we like My Week, but instead of sorting by the time view, we’d want to sort by workspace/board/group and filter by status.

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)

Suggestion: the My Week overview would be a more complete dashboard with a right pane. The right pane showing a list of the boards and the status (small view) of the items you are assigned on. The items in the right pane should lead you to a filtered board overview; showing only the items you are assigned on.

My-Week_right_pane3160×1642 668 KB

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Tpoint:

Which means that Monday does not care and will not provide this feature.

Not sure if that’s entirely true.

I’ve exchanged emails with Monday Support on this issue and they are adamant that they care but they’ve got a huge backlog of To-Do’s.

Whilst I sympathize with Monday.com, I have reviewed Feature Requests on this Community Forum over the past 12 months and I cannot identify one which has made its way onto the Roadmap and into the app. So I’m not clear how they’re prioritizing their new features.

I actually cannot find the Monday Roadmap any more since they removed the link to the last one a few weeks ago without explanation.

Hopefully we see some Feature Requests from these Community Forums implemented soon since they’ve got millions in VC funding in the bank and I’ve got a disgruntled team of Monday users who are craving immediate improvement to the core features to improve their productivity.

posts.author: Tpoint (Tobias)

But maybe they are just backlogged because they are using their own software and don’t have a central to do lists. And the searching / finding takes then so long.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Tpoint:

Why would lama farms be in but this feature not?

I hear you.

The priorities are a bit of a mystery at present. Subitems seem to be the current hot topic. I don’t mind gamification (llamas) of the UI if the core features are bulletproof.

On the flip side, gamification (counting llamas etc) is irritating when core functionality such as this is limited or missing.

posts.author: jiahan96 (Yeong Jia Han)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just saw this thread and am going to give my two cents here. I’m actually utilizing the battery function on my Dashboard as an alternative to see my “To-Do’s” across multiple boards.

I believe it can work by just selecting the boards you have your to-do’s on, subsequently filtering the items by a column value to appear by the necessary parameters (i.e. for Board A, “People = Me”, Timeline = Current + Overdue, ; Board B, “People = Me”, Status = In-Progress)

The only limitation to this is that I can have only a maximum of 10 boards hooked up per dashboard widget, but that can easily be solved by segregating my boards into different batteries. Still a win because this totally checks out for projects that involves multiple boards.

Main point is by doing this, I can my tasks on all boards based on the columns that are, by-nature, used to assign myself to work on it. Am I making sense?

posts.author: yasminmfn (Yasmin Nobbs)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julia-monday.com Is there any update on this feature?

posts.author: steckinsights (Brian Steck)
posts.cookedHtml:
For the record, I’m very on board for this feature being implemented.

In the meantime, I figured I’d offer two possible temporary solutions that may help.

Edit the status of the default blank status on your board(s) and change the title to “Incomplete” or “Unfinished” or “To-Do” (whatever feels right for you). Then you can search or filter for that term. It’s not a permanent fix because it’s not as quick as typing “-Done” or to show everything except the Done status, but it may help if you combine it with the filters and save a filter to show all statuses except Done.

The other temporary help in seeing just your active tasks (which doesn’t require changing the default gray status to have a name) is to use the automations to archive, delete or file “Done” status items when the pulse is changed.

Monday - you’ve got this! I believe in you. Just a simple negative filter would make us cry with joy

posts.author: jdesign (Justin)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed, this is huge for our workflow. We need our team members to be able to quickly see what items are still open for them to do. Just a simple checkbox in My Week to not include done items, or the ability to exclude done items in the search would solve this.

posts.author: Toploader (Jon Merchant)
posts.cookedHtml:
We needed something like this so badly that we ended up making groups for each person, and having the tasks in a list that way, then when a task is marked as done, we have an automation that moves it to another a ‘completed’ group. We have to do this for each board so It’s obviously not ideal, especially as we can’t set permissions on groups like you can on boards, but it shows how important it is for our users that we’re willing give up various other functionality in order to have clear and ordered to-do lists, at a glance.

posts.author: vishwajeets (Vishwajeet Singh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Here’s my nifty solution to this problem helping users to focus on their immediate priorities across boards.
Get things done App provides a dashboard widget which helps you to focus on items which are going to be due soon across the board. Users can define their own rules specifying which fields to track status and due date. Users can set their definition of done by choosing status column values, these items will be treated as completed hence won’t show in users My Work Items. Rules are specific to the user hence each user can use the same dashboard widget to define their own rules. Idea is to have a place for users to track items which they need to focus on in the immediate future from different boards. They don’t need to toggle across boards to find items which need their attention. Updates of items can be seen by simply clicking on item cards.

Currently we show items to which the user is subscribed to by default. In future releases, I intend to allow the user to pick an owner column and he can choose whether to see subscribed items or not.

Do checkout and let me know what do you guys think

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @vishwajeets

Looking very good! Two questions:

  • Will it show all items or only items where I am assigned to? In case of all items it would be wonderful if you add a filter for user (me only) or even select other users (in case you are the admin) to see their work to be done
  • Can I select all boards in account as an option in the boards dropdown?

Very nicely done.

posts.author: vishwajeets (Vishwajeet Singh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @basdebruin thank you for your kind words.

basdebruin:

Will it show all items or only items where I am assigned to? In case of all items it would be wonderful if you add a filter for user (me only) or even select other users (in case you are the admin) to see their work to be done

Currently it shows items subscribed by current user only, I am adding option to pick a column for owner/assignee so it will be able so items based on user in column instead of subscriber.

basdebruin:
  • Can I select all boards in account as an option in the boards dropdown?

Technically you can select all the boards which you have access to but you will have to add rule for each indvidually as it’s possible to have different status/date columns on different boards so need a way to have that mapping for each board. This can be given as an option if all boards have common status/date field, need to think over the possibility.

posts.author: TuesdaysTools (Tomorrow's Tools)
posts.cookedHtml: @alejencia thanks for sharing a description of your issue - we felt the same pain. Sounds like the TuesdaysTools feature that would help here are saved views. So as the Hiring Manager, they could save a view that only shows their tasks. But also someone managing the whole process could save a view called “Hiring Manager View” and see the board as they would see it. With our tool you can save any number of views and even share saved views across users. Hope this helps! We have a YouTube video above in this thread that goes over these features. There’s a link in the YouTube video description to our subscribe page if you’re interested in learning more.

thanks

Thanks again, really fantastic app

posts.author: scooper (Simeon Cooper)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Foobart, completely agree, Monday need to make the My Week section include all the columns of the tasks, allow filtering of everything working live on everything assigned to you across numerous boards in the one place rather than having to go to every board to update the tasks assigned to you. They could also add a project column to allow anything assigned on one board to be completely mirrored on a central ‘your to do list board’ as Asana do and as I have suggested as a needed function below, check it out!

https://community.monday.com/t/mirror-a-task-on-multiple-boards-with-a-project-column/6049

posts.author: scooper (Simeon Cooper)
posts.cookedHtml: This is great but its unfortunately just another additional fee for something Monday.com should be able to solve themselves and do better. Why can’t Monday.com just make this already!

posts.author: TuesdaysTools (Tomorrow's Tools)
posts.cookedHtml:
That’s why they created the App Marketplace - features that seem super obvious to some would be unnecessary for others. With the App Marketplace Monday effectively expands their development team 10x and we independent parties can solve market needs much faster.

And to Monday’s credit, they have made attempts to solve this with My Week and the like, but some people need more powerful tools. That’s where we come in. https://www.tomorrows-tools.com/

posts.author: zaneta (Zaneta Grant)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi everyone! I’m pleased to let you all know that our product team is working on replacing My Week with a feature called My Work that should include the ability to see only the tasks assigned to you and to choose which columns to filter out. We don’t have an exact ETA yet, but we’re hoping to roll it out later this year, so stay tuned. Thank you so much for your feedback so far!

Monday.com My Work Beta Issues&Improvements1908×940 106 KB

posts.author: Lindseyc (Lindsey Cultrera)
posts.cookedHtml:
This feature is basically a watered down version of ToDoist with less functionality. It’s absolutely not what people have been asking for. It might help the most basic user, but for executives, people with large teams or who work between multiple teams, or just larger companies, this doesn’t come anywhere near cutting it. You have multiple pages in this community describing in detail exactly what we need. I see the same functionality described and asked for over and over again, when is this going to get addressed? This makes me glad I only signed my company up for a month to month plan to do beta testing on whether it’s right for us because I’m seriously considering whether Asana would better meet our needs.

I’ve been calling this in our beta test group in my company a Command Center Page. We need a page that is fully customizable like a board, that we can push items to from ALL our other boards and determine what pushes it, whether it’s pushed to that Command Center Page based on it being assigned to you, or belonging to a specific project type, or status. I’m particularly looking to be able to group items on this command center page by Priority/Urgency as it’s for an executive and team who doesn’t usually work with solid due dates, and is working on a huge number of projects at once. If this isn’t possible we’ll have to cut our losses and switch to Asana now before we invest more in Monday.com. Like I said, I’ve seen this and many similar iterations of this, described and asked for all over the community boards, @zaneta Is this being worked on at all at this functionality level??

posts.author: Lindseyc (Lindsey Cultrera)
posts.cookedHtml: This is awesome! is it possible to change the view into lines items similar the boards, instead of block items?

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )

H Community!

posts.author: tyler2 (Tyler McCuen)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s incredibly dumb you cant move anything around in my work. it would be nice to have a kanban view for my work.
